summary: Ukrainian forces are increasingly striking Russian rear areas at distances of 40-100 km from the front line, using both mass-produced and foreign-made loitering munitions, with operators targeting vehicles and personnel deep behind the lines. Russian forces are countering with electronic warfare, increasing antenna sizes on REB systems and reducing FPV control frequencies, while also using motorcycle assaults and deceptive tactics such as fake surrenders to evade drone strikes. Ukrainian units, including the 36th Marine Brigade, are surviving FPV attacks using drone detectors, and Russian operators report destroying 186 Ukrainian targets in a single day across multiple directions. Meanwhile, new Ukrainian drone systems like the XDOWN STUD offer high speed (300 km/h), 64 km range, and 800 g payload, with emerging swarm and AI capabilities.
